title: Supreme Court Declares Right to Walk on Footpath a Fundamental Right, Calls for Legislative Framework
summary: The Supreme Court of India has declared that the right to walk on demarcated footpaths is a fundamental right of citizens. The court expressed concern over encroachment of footpaths by vendors and vehicles, which denies pedestrians safe passage. It called upon the legislature and concerned authorities to enact a specific law safeguarding pedestrians' right to use footpaths. The ruling connects the right to safe pedestrian movement to the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to expansion of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty) through judicial interpretation; tests understanding of how the Supreme Court reads fundamental rights expansively. Also relevant to GS Paper 2 (Judiciary/Constitutional Law).
key_facts: Court: Supreme Court of India
Fundamental Right invoked: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Direction: Legislature called upon to enact specific pedestrian protection law
Issue: Encroachment of footpaths denying safe pedestrian access

title: Reliance Jio Files for India's Largest-Ever IPO, Expected at ₹35,000–₹40,000 Crore
summary: Reliance Jio Infocomm has filed draft papers for an Initial Public Offering (IPO) that is expected to be valued between ₹35,000 crore and ₹40,000 crore, making it potentially the largest IPO in Indian history. The filing marks a significant milestone for India's capital markets and the telecom sector. Jio is a subsidiary of Reliance Industries Limited, promoted by Mukesh Ambani. The IPO is expected to draw substantial domestic and foreign investor interest given Jio's dominant position in India's telecom market.
exam_angle: Relevant to capital markets, SEBI regulations, and India's telecom sector; tests knowledge of IPO mechanisms, significance of primary markets for capital formation, and the role of large corporates in India's economy.
key_facts: Company: Reliance Jio Infocomm (subsidiary of Reliance Industries)
Expected IPO size: ₹35,000–₹40,000 crore
Context: Potentially India's largest-ever IPO
Regulator: SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India)

title: US Removes Social Media Post on India Trade Deal Featuring Map Showing PoK and Aksai Chin Outside India
summary: The United States government removed a social media post related to the India-US trade deal after it featured an inaccurate map of India that showed Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) and Aksai Chin as not being part of Indian territory. India has consistently maintained that both PoK and Aksai Chin are integral parts of Indian territory. The incident occurred amid ongoing India-US trade negotiations and drew swift diplomatic attention from New Delhi. This highlights India's firm stance on territorial integrity in all bilateral and multilateral engagements.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's foreign policy on territorial integrity, the India-US bilateral trade relationship, and the significance of cartographic accuracy in diplomatic contexts. Tests awareness of PoK and Aksai Chin disputes.
key_facts: Incorrect map: Showed PoK and Aksai Chin outside Indian territory
Post removed by: US government
Context: India-US trade deal discussions
India's position: Both PoK and Aksai Chin are integral parts of India

title: India and Uzbekistan Aim to Double Bilateral Trade Within Three Years
summary: India and Uzbekistan have announced their intent to double bilateral trade within three years as part of deepening economic ties between the two countries. The engagement is part of India's broader Connect Central Asia policy and outreach to Central Asian nations. Uzbekistan is a strategically important partner for India in the context of the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) and access to Central Asian markets. Both sides are also exploring enhanced connectivity, including the Chabahar port route.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's Connect Central Asia policy, INSTC, and trade diplomacy with landlocked Central Asian nations. May also be linked to SCO context and India's energy and connectivity interests.
key_facts: Target: Double bilateral trade within 3 years
Partners: India and Uzbekistan
Strategic relevance: India's Connect Central Asia Policy, INSTC
Connectivity route: Chabahar Port (Iran) enables access to Central Asia

title: NIXI Celebrates 23rd Foundation Day: India's Internet Exchange Infrastructure Milestone
summary: The National Internet Exchange of India (NIXI) marked its 23rd Foundation Day, highlighting its role in strengthening India's internet infrastructure. NIXI is a not-for-profit company under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) that facilitates the exchange of domestic internet traffic within India, reducing dependence on international bandwidth. NIXI also manages the .IN country-code Top-Level Domain (ccTLD) registry and IDN (Internationalized Domain Names). Its work supports the government's Digital India initiative by improving internet access, latency, and cost-efficiency.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's digital infrastructure, Digital India policy, and the role of MeitY in internet governance. NIXI is a frequently asked organisation in the context of India's internet ecosystem.
key_facts: Full form: National Internet Exchange of India (NIXI)
Founded: 2003 (23rd Foundation Day in 2026)
Ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
Key functions: Domestic internet traffic exchange, .IN domain registry, IDN management

title: Bengaluru Figures in Global Startup Hubs List, Reinforcing Its 'Silicon Valley of India' Status
summary: Bengaluru, often referred to as India's Silicon Valley, has been recognised in a global list of leading startup hubs. The city consistently ranks among the top startup ecosystems globally due to its concentration of IT companies, venture capital, research institutions, and skilled talent. This recognition underlines India's growing importance in the global innovation and entrepreneurship landscape. The Startup India initiative by the Government of India has contributed significantly to creating an enabling environment for startups nationwide.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's startup ecosystem, Startup India initiative, and innovation indices; connects to GS Paper 3 topics on Indian economy, industrial policy, and technology.
key_facts: City recognised: Bengaluru (Karnataka)
Known as: India's Silicon Valley
Government initiative: Startup India (launched January 2016)
Nodal Ministry for Startup India: DPIIT, Ministry of Commerce and Industry

title: Smaller AI Models Driving Escalation of Deepfake Threat in India
summary: A report highlights that smaller, more accessible Artificial Intelligence models are increasingly being used to create deepfakes in India, posing serious threats to individuals, democracy, and national security. Unlike large models requiring significant computing power, smaller AI models can run on consumer-grade hardware, making deepfake creation widely accessible. The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has been working on guidelines and regulations to address AI-generated misinformation. India's IT Act and proposed Digital India Act are relevant legal frameworks in this context.
exam_angle: Relevant to AI regulation, cybersecurity, and digital governance in India; connects to discussions on the Digital India Act, IT Act Section 66D, and MeitY's AI governance framework.
key_facts: Threat: Deepfakes generated using smaller, consumer-accessible AI models
Nodal Ministry: MeitY (Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology)
Relevant law: Information Technology Act, 2000 (Section 66D on impersonation)
Proposed framework: Digital India Act (to replace IT Act)

title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper to Leave Google DeepMind for Anthropic
summary: John Jumper, who was awarded the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for his pioneering work on protein structure prediction using AlphaFold at Google DeepMind, is set to leave the organisation and join Anthropic, an AI safety company. AlphaFold, developed by DeepMind, revolutionised structural biology by accurately predicting the three-dimensional structures of proteins. Anthropic, co-founded by former OpenAI researchers, focuses on AI safety research and is the creator of the Claude AI assistant. This move underlines the intense competition for top AI talent globally.
exam_angle: Relevant to Nobel Prize 2024 in Chemistry, AI developments, and protein structure prediction (AlphaFold); tests awareness of key persons in science and technology.
key_facts: Person: John Jumper — 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry (co-recipient)
Achievement: Development of AlphaFold (protein structure prediction AI) at Google DeepMind
Moving to: Anthropic (AI safety company, creator of Claude AI)
AlphaFold significance: Accurately predicts 3D protein structures, transforming biology and drug discovery

title: Trump Praises PM Modi, Plans Visits to Turkey and China in 2025
summary: US President Donald Trump stated his intention to visit both Turkey and China during 2025, signalling active engagement with key global powers. In a separate statement, Trump identified PM Narendra Modi and Chinese President Xi Jinping as world leaders he most admires, calling Modi a 'great leader' who 'stays out of wars'. Trump's appreciation for Modi's foreign policy posture reflects the ongoing positive trajectory of India-US relations. These diplomatic signals are significant amid ongoing global geopolitical realignments, including the Russia-Ukraine conflict and India-Pakistan tensions.
exam_angle: Relevant to India-US bilateral relations, Trump administration's foreign policy, and India's strategic autonomy; connects to GS Paper 2 topics on India's foreign policy and bilateral/multilateral relations.
key_facts: Trump's planned visits: Turkey and China (2025)
Leaders admired by Trump: PM Narendra Modi and Xi Jinping
Trump on Modi: 'Great leader, stays out of wars'
Context: India-US relations and India's strategic autonomy policy
